title: Events, Held State, Actions, Remapping, Focus, and Devices · Runtime Lens: Edge Held Actions
summary: The ship fires every frame a key is held, misses quit events, keeps thrust after focus loss, hard-codes WASD, assumes one controller identity, and requires precise simultaneous presses. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the edge held actions increment in device-independent remappable action layer. Learners progress through prediction, explic…
objectives: Map press, release, repeat, held, analog, and one-shot behavior to named actions with testable transitions.
Drain or pump the bounded event queue every frame and distinguish ordered events from sampled device state.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates.

Build deterministic Python evidence to separate discrete events from held actions while preserving remapping, focus-loss release, and device-independent intent; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. content.code:
# Worked focus: asteroids-edge-held-actions — Map press, release, repeat, held, analog, and one-shot behavior to named actions with testable transitions.
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-input-events-remapping-2
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-input-events-remapping-2.
# Case 7417: a headless continuous-integration replay.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state.
def worked_asteroids_input_events_remapping_2(event_frames: int = 6, pumped_frames: int = 6, actions_bound: int = 5, duplicate_bindings: int = 0, focus_lost_released: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "playerworked2",
        "seed7417",
        "input2dekrow",
        "frameworked27417",
        "state7417worked2",
        "playtest2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if event_frames < 1 or actions_bound < 3:
        return False, actions_bound, "input-shape"
    if pumped_frames != event_frames:
        return False, pumped_frames, "event-starvation"
    if duplicate_bindings > 0:
        return False, duplicate_bindings, "binding-conflict"
    if not focus_lost_released:
        return False, actions_bound, "stuck-input"
    assert pumped_frames == event_frames
    return True, actions_bound, "remappable-input"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python Asteroids decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected frame, input, seam, collision, cue, save, package, or release case, then state which native pygame-ce, SDL, display, controller, audio, filesystem, GPU, accessibility playtest, installer, performance, recovery, or publication claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-edge-held-actions-9y0dtn
# Competency: asteroids-edge-held-actions.
# Case 2057: a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling.

def evidence_asteroids_edge_held_actions_9y0dtn(event_frames: int = 6, pumped_frames: int = 6, actions_bound: int = 5, duplicate_bindings: int = 0, focus_lost_released: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:

        "player9y0dtn",
        "seed2057",
        "inputntd0y9",
        "frame9y0dtn2057",
        "state20579y0dtn",
        "playtestntd0y92057",

    assert len(set(evidence_axes_9y0dtn)) == 6
    if event_frames < 1 or actions_bound < 3:
        return False, actions_bound, "input-shape"
    if pumped_frames != event_frames:
        return False, pumped_frames, "event-starvation"
    if duplicate_bindings > 0:
        return False, duplicate_bindings, "binding-conflict"
    if not focus_lost_released:
        return False, actions_bound, "stuck-input"
    assert pumped_frames == event_frames
    return True, actions_bound, "remappable-input"
